root_text.an2.180-184:1.3: Kodho ca upanāho ca … makkho ca paḷāso ca … issā ca macchariyañca … māyā ca sāṭheyyañca … ahirikañca anottappañca.
root_text.an2.185-189:1.3: Akkodho ca anupanāho ca … amakkho ca apaḷāso ca … anissā ca amacchariyañca … amāyā ca asāṭheyyañca … hirī ca ottappañca.
root_text.an2.190-194:1.3: Kodhena ca upanāhena ca … makkhena ca paḷāsena ca … issāya ca macchariyena ca … māyāya ca sāṭheyyena ca … ahirikena ca anottappena ca.
root_text.an2.190-194:1.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, dvīhi dhammehi samannāgato dukkhaṁ viharati”.
root_text.an2.195-199:1.3: Akkodhena ca anupanāhena ca … amakkhena ca apaḷāsena ca … anissāya ca amacchariyena ca … amāyāya ca asāṭheyyena ca … hiriyā ca ottappena ca.
root_text.an2.195-199:1.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, dvīhi dhammehi samannāgato sukhaṁ viharati”.
root_text.an2.200-204:1.1: “Dveme, bhikkhave, dhammā sekhassa bhikkhuno parihānāya saṁvattanti.
root_text.an2.200-204:1.3: Kodho ca upanāho ca … makkho ca paḷāso ca … issā ca macchariyañca … māyā ca sāṭheyyañca … ahirikañca anottappañca.
root_text.an2.200-204:1.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, dve dhammā sekhassa bhikkhuno parihānāya saṁvattanti”.
root_text.an2.205-209:1.1: “Dveme, bhikkhave, dhammā sekhassa bhikkhuno aparihānāya saṁvattanti.
root_text.an2.205-209:1.3: Akkodho ca anupanāho ca … amakkho ca apaḷāso ca … anissā ca amacchariyañca … amāyā ca asāṭheyyañca … hirī ca ottappañca.
root_text.an2.205-209:1.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, dve dhammā sekhassa bhikkhuno aparihānāya saṁvattanti”.
root_text.an2.210-214:1.1: “Dvīhi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato yathābhataṁ nikkhitto evaṁ niraye.
root_text.an2.210-214:1.3: Kodhena ca upanāhena ca … makkhena ca paḷāsena ca … issāya ca macchariyena ca … māyāya ca sāṭheyyena ca … ahirikena ca anottappena ca.
root_text.an2.210-214:1.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, dvīhi dhammehi samannāgato yathābhataṁ nikkhitto evaṁ niraye”.
root_text.an2.215-219:1.1: “Dvīhi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato yathābhataṁ nikkhitto evaṁ sagge.
root_text.an2.215-219:1.3: Akkodhena ca anupanāhena ca … amakkhena ca apaḷāsena ca … anissāya ca amacchariyena ca … amāyāya ca asāṭheyyena ca … hiriyā ca ottappena ca.
root_text.an2.215-219:1.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, dvīhi dhammehi samannāgato yathābhataṁ nikkhitto evaṁ sagge”.
root_text.an2.220-224:1.1: “Dvīhi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato idhekacco kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an2.220-224:1.3: Kodhena ca upanāhena ca … makkhena ca paḷāsena ca … issāya ca macchariyena ca … māyāya ca sāṭheyyena ca … ahirikena ca anottappena ca.
root_text.an2.220-224:1.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, dvīhi dhammehi samannāgato idhekacco kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjati”.
root_text.an2.225-229:1.1: “Dvīhi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato idhekacco kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an2.225-229:1.3: Akkodhena ca anupanāhena ca … amakkhena ca apaḷāsena ca … anissāya ca amacchariyena ca … amāyāya ca asāṭheyyena ca … hiriyā ca ottappena ca.
root_text.an2.225-229:1.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, dvīhi dhammehi samannāgato idhekacco kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjati”.

translation_text.an2.180-184:0.2: The Chapter of Abbreviated Texts Beginning With Anger
translation_text.an2.180-184:1.3: Anger and acrimony … disdain and contempt … jealousy and stinginess … deceit and deviousness … lack of conscience and prudence.
translation_text.an2.185-189:1.3: Freedom from anger and acrimony … freedom from disdain and contempt … freedom from jealousy and stinginess … freedom from deceit and deviousness … conscience and prudence.
translation_text.an2.190-194:1.1: “Anyone who has two things lives in suffering.
translation_text.an2.190-194:1.3: Anger and acrimony … disdain and contempt … jealousy and stinginess … deceit and deviousness … lack of conscience and prudence.
translation_text.an2.190-194:1.4: Anyone who has these two things lives in suffering.”
translation_text.an2.195-199:1.1: “Anyone who has these two things lives happily.
translation_text.an2.195-199:1.3: Freedom from anger and acrimony … freedom from disdain and contempt … freedom from jealousy and stinginess … freedom from deceit and deviousness … conscience and prudence.
translation_text.an2.195-199:1.4: Anyone who has these two things lives happily.”
translation_text.an2.200-204:1.1: “These two things lead to the decline of a mendicant trainee.
translation_text.an2.200-204:1.3: Anger and acrimony … disdain and contempt … jealousy and stinginess … deceit and deviousness … lack of conscience and prudence.
translation_text.an2.200-204:1.4: These two things lead to the decline of a mendicant trainee.”
translation_text.an2.205-209:1.1: “These two things don’t lead to the decline of a mendicant trainee.
translation_text.an2.205-209:1.3: Freedom from anger and acrimony … freedom from disdain and contempt … freedom from jealousy and stinginess … freedom from deceit and deviousness … conscience and prudence.
translation_text.an2.205-209:1.4: These two things don’t lead to the decline of a mendicant trainee.”
translation_text.an2.210-214:1.1: “Anyone who has two things is placed in hell as if delivered there.
translation_text.an2.210-214:1.3: Anger and acrimony … disdain and contempt … jealousy and stinginess … deceit and deviousness … lack of conscience and prudence.
translation_text.an2.210-214:1.4: Anyone who has these two things is placed in hell as if delivered there.”
translation_text.an2.215-219:1.1: “Anyone who has two things is placed in heaven as if delivered there.
translation_text.an2.215-219:1.3: Freedom from anger and acrimony … freedom from disdain and contempt … freedom from jealousy and stinginess … freedom from deceit and deviousness … conscience and prudence.
translation_text.an2.215-219:1.4: Anyone who has these two things is placed in heaven as if delivered there.”
translation_text.an2.220-224:1.1: “When they have two things, some people, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a place of loss, a bad place, the underworld, hell.
translation_text.an2.220-224:1.3: Anger and acrimony … disdain and contempt … jealousy and stinginess … deceit and deviousness … lack of conscience and prudence.
translation_text.an2.220-224:1.4: When they have two things, some people, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a place of loss, a bad place, the underworld, hell.”
translation_text.an2.225-229:1.1: “When they have two things, some people—when their body breaks up, after death—are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.
translation_text.an2.225-229:1.3: Freedom from anger and acrimony … freedom from disdain and contempt … freedom from jealousy and stinginess … freedom from deceit and deviousness … conscience and prudence.
translation_text.an2.225-229:1.4: When they have these two things, some people—when their body breaks up, after death—are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.”

reference_text.an2.215-219:1.1: csp1ed15.96, csp2ed15.96, ms15A2_283, msdiv188, pts-vp-pli1.97.1, sya20.122
